metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Broaden the AC2 grep specification to cover paraphrased forms of the deprecated model (e.g. 'both votes.*green', 'votes.*pass', 'votes are.*+1', 'both.*+1.*submit') and enumerate at minimum the three known locations (your-first-change.md steps 5/7, README.md:288, infra/runbooks/github-mirror-lock.md:234-238) as required update targets alongside the grep.
findings.finding: AC2's grep pattern ('two green votes' / 'watch votes') is too narrow to catch all instances of the deprecated agent contract in the live docs. Real occurrences use different phrasing: your-first-change.md:98,103 says 'both votes are green' / 'Your job is done when both votes are green', and README.md:288 says 'submit once both votes pass'. A literal grep for only the two specified terms would miss these instances, giving the implementer false confidence the sweep is complete while the deprecated mental model survives in agent-visible documents.
findings.location: AC2 — 'Grep the whole repo for the deprecated "two green votes" / "watch votes" pattern'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the AC2 grep pattern with: `grep -rE '(two|both) (votes|bots).*green|(green|\+1).*(votes|bots)|watch.*votes|votes.*green|submit once both' docs/ CLAUDE.md CONTRIBUTING.md README.md infra/runbooks/` and add a sentence enumerating at minimum docs/your-first-change.md (steps 5,7), README.md (line 288), and infra/runbooks/github-mirror-lock.md (§5) as known instances that must be updated.
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: AC2 instructs the executor to grep the repo and update all deprecated-pattern occurrences, but provides no post-update verification command to confirm zero occurrences remain — the completeness of the sweep is claimed but unmeasured.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria – AC2 ('Grep the whole repo…')
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC2: 'After updating, run `grep -rn --include="*.md" "two green votes\|watch votes\|treat.*green.*land" .` and confirm zero matches before marking done.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `README.md` to the edit-target list (file_impact) and include it in the whole-repo deprecated-pattern sweep mandated by AC2.
findings.finding: README.md contains the deprecated 'both votes pass' phrasing (line 288: 'submit once both votes pass') but is not listed in the plan's file_impact or named as an edit target, despite AC2 mandating that ALL repo occurrences of the deprecated pattern be updated.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `{"path": "README.md", "reason": "deprecated two-votes pattern at line 288"}` to file_impact, and update README.md line 288 to reference `land` / `Autosubmit` as the new landing mechanism rather than 'both votes pass'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand the 'What' section to explicitly call out the whole-repo grep-and-replace of deprecated 'two green votes'/'watch votes' patterns as a named deliverable alongside the CLAUDE.md/CONTRIBUTING.md rewrite.
findings.finding: The 'What' section describes only rewriting 'this repo's landing section in CLAUDE.md + CONTRIBUTING.md' but AC2 mandates a whole-repo grep and update of ALL deprecated occurrences (e.g. docs/your-first-change.md). The broader scope of the whole-repo audit is not mentioned anywhere in the plan body, leaving an uncovered deliverable in the description and potentially surprising the implementer.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to 'What': 'Additionally, audit the entire repo for any remaining references to the deprecated two-green-votes / watch-votes pattern (e.g. docs/your-first-change.md) and update them to the new contract.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out note (e.g. 'if S1 or S4 is reverted post-merge, revert this doc change via git revert <sha> and re-issue once tooling stabilizes') so the sequencing constraint has a named recovery path.
findings.finding: The plan acknowledges that changing the agent landing contract is a breaking change and sequences the merge after S1+S4 go live, but it states no explicit rollback or back-out mechanism if the merge sequencing fails (e.g. docs land before tooling is live, or tooling is reverted after docs land). The T4 rubric requires an explicit rollback/back-out path, not just acknowledgement of the break.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' subsection under Dependencies: 'If S1 (Autosubmit label) or S4 (land command) is reverted after this doc change has landed, immediately revert this change via git revert and pin a TODO to re-land once the tooling stabilizes.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to the ADR-0042 AC a concrete verification step: specify the command/query used to confirm each idea ID resolves in the store, and add a fallback action (e.g., 'if unresolvable, record a `BROKEN-LINK` TODO inline and open a tracking ticket') so implementers have unambiguous guidance.
findings.finding: The AC requires 'Confirm both idea ids resolve in the store' for deferral anchors 1701-8bc1-e3b7-4984 and c3a9-5f4c-bdc6-4e27, but the plan specifies no mechanism, tooling reference, or fallback action if one or both IDs fail to resolve. An implementer has no guidance on how to perform this check or what to do on failure — if the IDs are stale the ADR will contain broken forward-pointers, undermining its stated goal of letting future idea-epics 'inherit the why.'
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — ADR-0042 bullet: 'Confirm both idea ids resolve in the store and anchor ADR-0042 to them.'
findings.suggested_fix: Amend the AC bullet to read: 'Confirm both idea ids resolve in the store by running `<store-lookup-command> 1701-8bc1-e3b7-4984` and `<store-lookup-command> c3a9-5f4c-bdc6-4e27` before drafting the ADR. If either is unresolvable, add an inline `<!-- BROKEN-LINK: id=<id> — open tracking ticket -->` comment and file a linking ticket; do not silently omit the deferral anchor.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a requirement that `CLAUDE.md`/`CONTRIBUTING.md` enumerate the outcome enum (e.g., success, conflict, failure, timeout) and specify the required agent action for each non-happy-path outcome, not only the prohibition on vote-watching.
findings.finding: The CLAUDE.md/CONTRIBUTING.md rewrite AC specifies only the happy-path and the negative constraint ('do NOT watch votes'), but does not require the docs to enumerate what agents must do for each non-happy-path outcome (e.g., conflict detected, land command fails, autoheal timeout). Agents encountering a failure outcome have no documented remediation path in the landing section.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — first bullet: "`CLAUDE.md` + `CONTRIBUTING.md` landing section rewritten: 'set `Autosubmit` / run `land`; act on the single typed outcome; do NOT watch votes...'"
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the first AC bullet to read: '...act on the single typed outcome; for each non-success outcome (conflict, infra-failure, hang/timeout) the docs MUST state the exact remediation step the agent should take (e.g., resolve conflict and re-run `land`; wait for autoheal; escalate after N minutes).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit, observable verification step for the two idea IDs (e.g. 'run `idea show 1701-8bc1-e3b7-4984` and `idea show c3a9-5f4c-bdc6-4e27` and paste the resolved titles into the ADR PR description') so reviewers can confirm resolution before merging.
findings.finding: AC item 3 requires 'Confirm both idea ids resolve in the store' as part of the ADR-0042 acceptance criteria, but the plan provides no observable verification mechanism — no query, no tool invocation, no owner. If the ids are stale or mistyped the ADR will silently carry broken references, and there is no stated check that would catch this before the ticket closes.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC item 3 (ADR-0042 content), sentence: 'Confirm both idea ids resolve in the store and anchor ADR-0042 to them.'
findings.suggested_fix: In AC item 3, replace 'Confirm both idea ids resolve in the store' with: 'Verify both idea ids resolve: include the output of `idea show <id>` (or equivalent) for each id in the ADR PR description as proof-of-resolution; the ADR must quote the resolved idea titles, not just the raw IDs.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief positive rationale sentence to the 'What' or 'Why' section explaining why a formal ADR with prior-art grounding is the right artifact (e.g. 'An ADR captures the eight key design decisions and their alternatives so future platform evaluations start from documented reasoning, not re-derivation; a whole-repo grep ensures the deprecated contract is fully purged rather than partially updated').
findings.finding: The plan's 'Why' section explains the problem motivation but provides no positive rationale for the chosen approach (structured ADR-0042 with eight named design decisions + prior-art grounding + whole-repo grep, rather than e.g. inline comments on each doc or a single canonical landing-contract page). For a docs plan the choices are less weighty, but the ADR itself represents real structural choices (what to record, what to defer, how to anchor deferrals) where a brief rationale sentence would confirm the author intentionally chose this form.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] AC1: require the rewritten CLAUDE.md/CONTRIBUTING.md landing section to explicitly cite `docs/land-contract.md` (the stable outcome-enum + exit-code + JSON-schema reference authored by S4) so agents have a single, named destination for the per-outcome decision rules.
findings.finding: AC1 requires CLAUDE.md/CONTRIBUTING.md to tell agents to 'act on the single typed outcome' but does not require the rewritten docs to reference docs/land-contract.md — the only place the outcome enum vocabulary (merged, needs_rebase, ci_failed, review_failed, abandoned, lander_down, timed_out, error, not_requested, pending) and per-outcome handling rules will be formally defined (S4 AC7). Without this pointer, an agent reading the rewritten docs knows to 'act on the outcome' but has no specified path to learn what the outcomes are or what action each demands.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC1: 'act on the single typed outcome; do NOT watch votes…'
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC1 to: '…act on the single typed outcome per `docs/land-contract.md` (the stable versioned contract — enum values, exit codes, JSON schema, and per-outcome agent action); do NOT watch votes…'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] AC1: add a requirement that the rewritten landing section includes (or links to) positive per-outcome agent actions — at minimum: what to do on `needs_rebase`, `ci_failed`, `review_failed`, `lander_down`, and `timed_out` — not only the negative 'do NOT watch votes' constraint.
findings.finding: AC1's instruction phrase is framed as a pink-elephant negative constraint ('do NOT watch votes or treat two green votes as "it will land"') without requiring the rewritten docs to enumerate the positive per-outcome agent decision rules (e.g. 'if needs_rebase → rebase to tip and rerun land; if ci_failed → fix CI and repush; if lander_down → follow FFO manual path'). The doc-writing specification only pins the forbidden behavior, leaving the required positive handling protocol unspecified at the landing section level.

AC1 instructs the rewritten docs to tell agents to 'act on the single typed outcome' but never requires the docs to enumerate the outcome values or the per-outcome decision rules. The outcome enum vocabulary (merged, needs_rebase, ci_failed, review_failed, abandoned, timed_out, error, pending) and what action each value demands (rebase vs fix CI vs fix review findings vs investigate) is defined in S4 / `docs/land-contract.md` but is not required to appear in or be cross-referenced from the rewritten CLAUDE.md/CONTRIBUTING.md landing section. This is an instruction-locality gap: the processing protocol is not co-located with the section that tells agents to use it.
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC1 to read: '…; act on the single typed outcome per the decision table in `docs/land-contract.md` (cross-link it inline); the rewritten section must enumerate or directly reference at minimum: merged → done; needs_rebase → rebase and re-push; ci_failed → inspect CI run; review_failed → address LLM-Review findings; timed_out/error → retry or escalate.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
